description.en: Shabbat of Repentance between Rosh Hashanah and Yom Kippur.

Shabbat HaGadol (“The Great Shabbat”) is the Shabbat that immediately precedes the holiday of Passover. Jewish texts offer many explanations as to why this Shabbat is called “great,” like those that  point to a great miracle performed on this Shabbat during the exodus, or those that point to the word “great” at the end of the haftarah prophetic reading traditionally read on this Shabbat. In many communities, the rabbi delivers a lengthy sermon on a Passover-related topic.
description.he: ״שבת הגדול״ היא השבת האחרונה לפני חג הפסח. שבת זו נודעה כשבת מיוחדת החל מהמאה ה-12 בבתי המדרש של רש״י וחכמי התוספות. מנהגי שבת זו כוללים דרשה של הרב בקהילתו בהלכות הפסח, וקריאת קטעים מההגדה של פסח. מספר טעמים ניתנו לשמה של שבת זו כגון שבשבת זו בני ישראל הפסיקו לעבוד עבודה זרה, או שהיה נס גדול בלקיחת השה לקרבן פסח, ועוד.

toTopic.description: Slug of the `toTopic` (i.e. the topic to which the link connects to, usually the slug passed as `topic_slug` to the `api/topic-graph` endpoint).

linkType.description:
The type of the link, based on the relationship within our ontology. Many links has an `is-a` relationship (such as `Genesis` `is-a` `Book`), but other topic-to-topic links have a variety of other types documented [here](https://developers.sefaria.org/docs/topic-ontology#overview-of-sefaria-link-types). 

Some examples include `child-of`, `parent-of`, `sibling-of` etc.

class.description: Class of the link `intraTopic` (a link from topic-to-topic) or `refTopic` (a link from a topic-to-ref).

dataSource.description: Source where the topic link originated from.

fromTopic.description: Slug of the `fromTopic` (i.e. the topic from which the link originates).

RandomByTopicJSON.title: Root Type for RandomByTopicJSON
RandomByTopicJSON.description: Response to the random-by-topic API endpoint.

ref.description: The `Ref` of the text randomly selected, which is connected to one of the recently popular topics.

url.description: The `Ref` in a format appropriate for a URL, with spaces replaced with `.` etc.

description.en: Hillel was one of the most influential and prolific of the early teachers. Coming from Babylon to learn with Avtalyon and Shemaya, he soon became famous for his scholarship and his patient and tolerant manner. Bolstered by his Davidic lineage, he served as Nasi during part of Herod’s reign.
description.he: הלל היה אחד החכמים הראשונים, ומהפורים והמשפיעים ביניהם. הוא הגיע מבבל כדי ללמוד עם שמעיה ואבטליון, ונודע מהר מאוד בשל חוכמתו בתורה ואופיו הסבלני והסובלני. היותו צאצא של בית דוד הייתה בסיס לכהונתו כנשיא בתקופת שלטונו של הורדוס.

slug.description: The unique slug for a given topic.

alt_ids.description: This is a dictionary containing the keys of naming schemes, and ids of this topic represented in that naming scheme.  It’s used both for canonical names in other naming schemes and temporary storage of names when renaming slugs. All of the data here is for internal use, with the exception of the values `bfo` and `wikidata`. `bfo` refers to [basic formal ontology](https://github.com/BFO-ontology/BFO#implementations), and `wikidata` to the wikidata ontology of topics.

categoryDescription.description: A description of the category of this topic

numSources.description: The number of text sources associated with a topic.

description_published.description: A boolean representing whether or not the description was published.

data_source.description: How the topic was generated, whether by Sefaria, a user on a source sheet, or some other source.

description.en: A mezuzah is the small parchment placed inside a case and attached to the right side of entryway doorframes where Jews reside. Written on the scroll are various sections from the Torah, such as the Shema, that feature some of Judaism's central principles.
description.he: המזוזה היא נייר קלף קטן הנמצא בתוך נרתיק (בית המזוזה) המחובר לצד הימני של משקוף הדלת בבתי יהודים. על מגילת הקלף כתובים מגוון קטעים מן התורה, דוגמת פרשת שמע הכוללת כמה מהעקרונות העיקריים של היהדות.

linkType.description:
The type of the link, based on the relationship within our ontology. Many links has an `is-a` relationship (such as `Genesis` `is-a` `Book`), but other topic-to-topic links have a variety of other types documented  [here](https://developers.sefaria.org/docs/topic-ontology#overview-of-sefaria-link-types). 

Some examples include `child-of`, `parent-of`, `sibling-of` etc.

class.description: A classification of the link as either `refTopic` (i.e. a ref to a topic) or `intraTopic` (topic-to-topic). In this case, it would be expected that all returned topic links would be of type `refTopic`.

is_sheet.description: Is this ref-to-topic link one that was generated by a user-created source sheet?

descriptions.description: JSON containing descriptions for a given topic in various languages. Each language contains a `title` for the topic description, as well as a `prompt` which previews the source and asks questions to help the user engage with the text.

example.title: A New Grain Offering
example.prompt: The central ritual of Shavuot as described in the Torah is a special bread offering that was brought to the Temple. This sacrifice marked the beginning of sacrifices that were brought using grain from that year, as opposed to grains from the previous year, as explained in the Book of Numbers.

anchorRef.description: The `Ref` the topic is connected to, which should overlap with the requested `Ref` passed through the endpoint. For example, if someone queries for `Bamidbar 28:21`, one of the topics returned is `passover` which has an `anchorRef` of `Numbers 28:16-24` - a range overlapping with our desired `Ref`.

anchorRefExpanded.description: If the `anchorRef` is a ranged `Ref` (i.e. containing more than a single segment), then this is a list of strings, with each string being one of single segment level `Ref`s that together comprise the range. So for example, the `anchorRefExpanded` of `Exodus 1:1-2` would be `["Exodus 1:1", "Exodus 1:2"]`.

en.title: A New Grain Offering
en.prompt: The central ritual of Shavuot as described in the Torah is a special bread offering that was brought to the Temple. This sacrifice marked the beginning of sacrifices that were brought using grain from that year, as opposed to grains from the previous year, as explained in the Book of Numbers.

DataSourceJSON.title: Root Type for DataSourceJSON
DataSourceJSON.description: JSON containing the information as to the source of the data for a given ref-to-topic link.

en.description: English title of the data source.

he.description: Hebrew title of the data source.

slug.description: Unique slug describing the data source.

OrderTopicLinkJSON.title: Root Type for OrderTopicLinkJSON
OrderTopicLinkJSON.description: This JSON includes the various metrics that are relevant for ordering the link on a topics page. We use these metrics at [Sefaria.org](sefaria.org) to change the order on the topics page.

tfidf.description: Results of the [tfidf algorithm](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tf%E2%80%93idf) run on the `Ref`s within a given topic, as another metric for evaluating the relevance of a given source on a topic page.

numDatasource.description: The number of datasources which connect this `Ref` to the topic. A value of `2` will occur when two sources (i.e. `aspaklaria` and `sefaria sheets` both have connections between this `Ref` and this topic.)

availableLangs.description: This topic is available in a list of languages represented as strings with their language code.

comp_date.description: The date when the book referenced in the `Ref` was composed. Negative numbers indicate the work was composed BCE, whereas positive numbers indicate the book was composed CE.

pr.description: `PageRank`. Sefaria calculates `PageRank` for the sources on a given topic page using the internal Sefaria links to create a link graph and then running the [PageRank algorithm](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/PageRank) on that. Sources with higher `PageRank` are considered to be more centrally linked and more relevant.

curatedPrimacy.description: This field is used for manually ordering a source on a topics page. It has a separate order value for each language.

TermsJSON.title: Root Type for TermsJSON
TermsJSON.description: Term JSON returned to a query for a Term.

name.description: The name of the `Term`. Since a `Term` is a shared title node that can be referenced and used by many different `Index` nodes, the `name` field is critical as it contains the shared title.

titles.description: Alternative titles for the term in Hebrew and English.

scheme.description: A shared `scheme` for a group of terms.

order.description: Terms that share a `scheme` can be ordered within that `scheme`. So for example, _Parshiyot_ within the `Parasha` scheme can be ordered as per the order of the _Parshiyot_.

category.description: The category of a specific term.

TitlesJSON.title: Root Type for TopicTitlesJSON
TitlesJSON.description: Alternative title for a topic, along with the language code representing the language of the given title.

lang.description: The language of the title, either `en` or `he`.

Primary.description: A field depicting whether or not the title is the primary title. Note: For any given topics, one should expect two titles with this field present and set to `true`, an English and a Hebrew primary title. The English value with `primary` set to `true` will match the string value of the `primaryTitle` field on topic.

links.description: An array of JSON containing the metadata for links to the reference.

sheets.description: All public source sheets linked to a given `Ref` or containing that `Ref` on the sheet.

notes.description: Any notes a user has on a given `Ref`. **Note:** This data is only available for logged-in users to view their own notes on a text. This field will always present as an empty array for third party developers.

description.en: Devarim (“Words”) is the first Torah portion in the Book of Deuteronomy, the final book of the Torah. In it, Moses recounts events from the Israelites’ travels in the desert, like the appointment of judges, the sin of the spies, and the wars with the Emorite kings Sihon and Og.
description.he: דברים היא הפרשה הראשונה בספר דברים, החומש החמישי והאחרון בתורה. בספר מובאים נאומי משה, שבהם הוא מזכיר אירועים מהעבר, סוקר מצוות שכבר ניתנו ומצווה מצוות חדשות. בפרשה משה מספר על הצורך במינוי שופטים להנהגת העם. הוא גם מזכיר את חטא המרגלים, שבו התלונן העם על הכניסה העתידית לארץ כנען, את העונש שהוטל על העם לנדוד במדבר ארבעים שנה ואת מלחמות ישראל במדבר.

WebPagesJSON.title: Root Type for WebPagesJSON
WebPagesJSON.description: A JSON object containing the metadata for external webpages linked to this Sefaria `Ref`.

url.description: URL to the related article on the webpage.

linkerHits.description: Number of hits by the Sefaria linker.

anchorRefExpanded.description: If the `anchorRef` is a ranged `Ref`, this is an array of `Refs` containing each of the segments contained within that range. So for example, if `anchorRef` is Genesis 1:1-2, then `anchorRefExpanded` will be `["Genesis 1:1", "Genesis 1:2"]`.

SheetsJSON.title: Root Type for SheetsJSON
SheetsJSON.description: JSON containing metadata relating for a given source sheet on [Sefaria](sefaria.org).

owner.description: the Sefaria User ID of the sheet's owner.

id.description: The `id` of the sheet. This is used in the URL to retrieve sheets, for example, a sheet with an `id` of 1, would be accessible on [Sefaria](sefaria.org) at [sefaria.org/sheets/1](sefaria.org/sheets/1).

anchorRefExpanded.description:
An array containing the individual segments `Ref`s which comprise the `anchorRef`. 

Example 1: Ranged `Ref`
If the `anchorRef` is `Genesis 2:14-16`, the `anchorRefExpanded` is `["Genesis 2:14", "Genesis 2:15", "Genesis 2:16"]`. 

Example 2: Segment Ref
If the `anchorRef` itself is a segment `Ref`, the `anchorRefExpanded` will contain that identical value. For example, `anchorRef` of `Genesis 2:11` will  have an `anchorRefExpanded` of `["Genesis 2:11"]`

options.description: A JSON object containing document level options related to how this source sheet should be displayed in Sefaria's Source Sheet editor.

numbered.description: Should the sources be automatically numbered in the Sefaria editor view

boxed.description: Should the sources be boxed on the page in the Sefaria editor view

assignable.description: Is this source sheet an assignment for students in the Sefaria editor view

bsd.description: Should the source sheet include בס"ד at the top of the page in the Sefaria editor view

language.description: Default display language of the source sheet in the Sefaria editor view

layout.description: Default display layout of the source sheet in the Sefaria editor view

langLayout.description: Default display side of the language in a bilingual side-by-side view in the Sefaria editor view

divineNames.description: Should the Tetragrammaton (Shem Hashem) and other variants of the divine name be replaced, and if so in what form?

ownerName.description: Name of the owner of the sheet.

via.description: Some source sheets are made by copying another users. If this is one of them, the original owner's user ID will be listed here.

viaOwnerName.description: Some source sheets are made by copying another users. If this is one of them, the original owner's display name will be listed here.

assignerName.description: Some source sheets are created as assignments by a teacher to be completed by students. If this is one of those sheets the name of the assigner will be found here.

viaOwnerProfileUrl.description: Some source sheets are made by copying another users. If this is one of them, the path to the original owner's profile page will be listed here.

assignerProfileUrl.description: Some source sheets are created as assignments by a teacher to be completed by students. If this is one of those sheets this will be the URL path to their profile.

ownerProfileUrl.description: The URL Path that will link to a user's profile on the Sefaria site.

status.description: a string indicating whether the sheet is `public` or `unlisted`

topics.description: Topics linked to the source sheet.

likes.description: An array of user IDs who have favorited or liked this source sheet.

summary.description: A user generated description of the source sheet

attribution.description: Some source sheets are made by copying another users. If so, an attribution field will be created and displayed here.

is_featured.description: Previously a source sheet flagged with `is_featured` would be promoted throughout the site in various places. This behavior was removed in 2022 but some of the data remains.

asTyped.description: How the user typed the topic

RelatedLinkJSON.title: Root Type for RelatedLinkJSON
RelatedLinkJSON.description: The link JSON returned as part of results for the related API endpoint.

index_title.description: English title of the linked work.

category.description: Category of the type of link, for example a link between `Rashi on Exodus` and `Exodus` would have a category of `Commentary`.

anchorRefExpanded.description:
An array of strings, where each string is a segment level `Ref` of the `Ref` passed in as a parameter. If a segment level `Ref` is passed in, this array will have a length of 1, and only contain that segment level `Ref`. If the `Ref` passed in is ranged, this array will contain each of the segment level `Ref`s within that range.

For example, if someone was searching for links for Esther 4.14-15, the `anchorRefExpanded` would contain Esther 4.14 and Esther 4.15.

sourceHasEn.description: Does the source have an English translation?

compDate.description: Date of the two books in the link were composed. Negative values indicate dates BCE.

heTitle.description: Hebrew title of the linked work

RelatedTopicJSON.title: Root Type for RelatedTopicJSON
RelatedTopicJSON.description: Topic JSON returned in the `topic` field of the related API endpoint.

linkType.description: The type of link between the topic and the `Ref`. For more on Sefaria's topic ontology, see [here](https://developers.sefaria.org/docs/topic-ontology).

class.description: A classification of the link as either `refTopic` (i.e. a ref to a topic) or `intraTopic` (topic-to-topic). In this case, it would be expected that all returned topic links would be of type `refTopic`.

is_sheet.description: Is this ref-to-topic link one that was generated by a user-created source sheet?

generatedBy.description: How the topic was generated (i.e. by a user, aspaklaria, Sefaria staff)

title.description: The title of the topic in Hebrew (`he`) and English (`en`).

titleIsTransliteration.description: A boolean value for the `he` and `en` fields inside the title JSON, indicating whether or not either translation is a transliteration.

description.description: JSON containing descriptions for a given topic in various languages. Each language contains a title for the topic description, as well as a prompt which previews the source and asks questions to help the user engage with the text.

example.en: Devarim (“Words”) is the first Torah portion in the Book of Deuteronomy, the final book of the Torah. In it, Moses recounts events from the Israelites’ travels in the desert, like the appointment of judges, the sin of the spies, and the wars with the Emorite kings Sihon and Og.
example.he: דברים היא הפרשה הראשונה בספר דברים, החומש החמישי והאחרון בתורה. בספר מובאים נאומי משה, שבהם הוא מזכיר אירועים מהעבר, סוקר מצוות שכבר ניתנו ומצווה מצוות חדשות. בפרשה משה מספר על הצורך במינוי שופטים להנהגת העם. הוא גם מזכיר את חטא המרגלים, שבו התלונן העם על הכניסה העתידית לארץ כנען, את העונש שהוטל על העם לנדוד במדבר ארבעים שנה ואת מלחמות ישראל במדבר.

RawIndexJSON.title: Root Type for RawIndexJSON
RawIndexJSON.description: The full mongo record JSON from the database for a given Sefaria `Index`.

categories.description: The categories within which this text falls. *Note:* The order of the strings in the array is significant. The categories should go from most general at position `0`, to most specific.

default_struct.description: Which structure of the available structures is default.

enDesc.description: A longer English description of the index.
enDesc.example: "Pesachim (“Passover Festivals”) is the third tractate in Seder Moed (“Festivals”). Its ten chapters discuss laws relating to Passover: the consumption of matzah (unleavened bread) and maror (bitter herbs), the prohibitions against owning or consuming chametz (leavened bread) on the festival, the paschal lamb that used to be offered at the Temple in Jerusalem, and the order of the seder (ritual feast) held on the first evening of the holiday.\n."

enShortDesc.description: A short English description of the index.
enShortDesc.example: Passover: ridding of chametz, the Paschal lamb offering, matzah, maror, and the Seder.

heShortDesc.description: A short description of the text in Hebrew.
heShortDesc.example: דינים הקשורים לחג הפסח: איסור חמץ, קורבן הפסח, מצה, מרור וליל הסדר.

pubDate.description: Date(s) of publication. *Note:* A negative value indicates the work was published BCE.

hasErrorMargin.description: This field tells you if `compDate` is list of 2 integers (i.e. is a range) because much of the time `compDate` will be a range only when there's an error margin in our knowledge of the completion date.

compDate.description:
Date(s) of text composition. 

**Notes:** 
- A negative value indicates BCE. 
- The integers are Gregorian years.
- If it has 2 values, that is the range of years it was composed.
- If has 1 value, that is the year it was composed.
- If it has 0, we don’t know the year it was composed.

era.description:
Era of the text. The value will be represented by one of the following codes. 

```
    "Gaonim": "GN",
    "Rishonim": "RI",
    "Achronim": "AH",
    "Tannaim": "T",
    "Amoraim": "A",
    "Contemporary": "CO"
```

is_cited.description: Indicator of whether ot not this text is cited by others in our library. Functionally, this means we will pick up links to this text when cited in other texts.

example.title: Pesachim
example.enDesc:
Pesachim (“Passover Festivals”) is the third tractate in Seder Moed (“Festivals”). Its ten chapters discuss laws relating to Passover: the consumption of matzah (unleavened bread) and maror (bitter herbs), the prohibitions against owning or consuming chametz (leavened bread) on the festival, the paschal lamb that used to be offered at the Temple in Jerusalem, and the order of the seder (ritual feast) held on the first evening of the holiday.
.
example.enShortDesc: Passover: ridding of chametz, the Paschal lamb offering, matzah, maror, and the Seder.
example.heShortDesc: דינים הקשורים לחג הפסח: איסור חמץ, קורבן הפסח, מצה, מרור וליל הסדר.

nodeType.description: The type of node. Nodes can either be `SchemaNodes` or `JaggedArray` nodes. A `JaggedArray` is a nested array (2D or 3D), whereas a `SchemaNode` is a node with children of either type. All tree leaves in the Sefaria text tree must be `JaggedArray` nodes.

depth.description: The depth of a text. For example, a book of Tanakh with chapters and verses has a depth 2, whereas a commentary on that book of Tanakh, with multiple comments on each verse (so a chapter-verse-comment structure) would have a depth of 3.

addressTypes.description: A list which length reflects the depth of the text structure, with string values indicating class names for address types for each level.

sectionNames.description: An array containing the type names of the sections of this text. The length of `sectionNames` gives the depth of the structure of this text. For example, Kohelet (`["Chapter", "Verse"]`) has depth 2, while Mishneh Torah (`["Book", "Topic", "Section", "Law"]`) has depth 4. Comparing this depth to the depth of sections will show if the request was for the lowest level, or a higher level, of the text.

match_templates.description: This data is used for the linker.

lengths.description:
The lengths of the text at each level of depth. For example, a text of depth 2 will have an array with two values, the first representing the number of sections, and the second value representing the number of verses. So, if Genesis has lengths, it's array will look like this `[50, 1533]`. This is interpreted to mean the Genesis Index has 50 chapters (sections) and 1533 verses (segments).

A depth-3 text (a commentary, for example) will have three values, representing the number of super sections, sections, and segments.

key.description: Unique key for the text, usually matches the `Index` title.

RawIndexAltStructsJSON.title: Root Type for RawIndexAltStructsJSON
RawIndexAltStructsJSON.description: The JSON returned for the alt structs of an Index.

numeric_equivalent.description: The numeric equivalent of the section. So for example, if chapter two of Masechet Pesachim is referred to as `כל שעה`, the `numeric_equivalent` is 2.

titles.description: Titles for this section of text.

FindRefsPOSTRequest.title: Root Type for FindRefsPOSTRequest
FindRefsPOSTRequest.description: A properly formatted POST request for the Find Refs API

text.description: Initially designed for websites, this API expects the `text` field to contain both a `body` and a `title`. When parsing arbitrary text feel free to just use `body` and pass an empty string `''` to `title`

lang.description: Either `he` or `en`. If not included, it will default to one based on the number of Hebrew or Latin characters in the `body` of the `text`.

text.body: Genesis 1:1 is a citation and also Mishnah Peah 3-4. So is שמות ב
text.title: Some thoughts on Sefer Bereishit 1

aggs.description: List of fields to aggregate on. Common fields are `path` for the `text` type and `group` or `topics` for the `sheet` type

field.description: The field you want to query. Common fields to query are `exact` or `naive_lemmatizer` for the `text` and `merged` indices. For querying the `sheet` index, commonly you'll query the `content` field

filter_fields.description: Must be the same length as `filters`. Each entry specifies the field to apply the corresponding filter in `filters`. For queries of type `text` this has no effect since there's only one field to filter text queries on (`path`. this field is explained in `filters`). For `sheet` queries, the following fields can appear in `filter_fields`: `collections` (corresponds to the collections that the sheet is in), `topics_en` (corresponds to the topics for this sheet, translated into English), `topics_he` (corresponds to the topics for this sheet, translated into Hebrew).

filters.description: A list of filters to filter results. These filters cannot include RegEx. Any RegEx characters will be escaped. Each filter is applied to the corresponding field in the `filter_fields` list. E.g. if filters is `["Passover", "Torah Talks"]` and `filter_fields` is `["topics_en", "collections"]` then the `"Passover"` filter will be applied to the `"topics_en"` field and the `"Torah Talks"` filter will be applied to the `"collections"` field. For `text` queries, filters always applies to the `path` field of documents. This essentially corresponds to the category path of the book in Sefaria's table of contents (there are some differences with regards to commentary paths). For `sheet` queries, filters can be applied to `collections`, `topics_en` or `topics_he`. These fields are explained in `filter_fields` param.

size.description: For paginating results. The total number of results to return, starting from `start`

slop.description: The maximum distance between each query word in the resulting document. `0` means an exact match must be found

sort_fields.description: List of fields to sort on. If `sort_method = 'score'` this list should have exactly one item. Common fields to sort on are `comp_date` (which list results from titles published chronologically), `order` (which list results based on Sefaria's table of contents structure), `pagesheetrank` (most relevant results based on the Sheet Rank algorithm), `dateCreated` (for sheet results returned chronologically), `views` (for sheet results based on popularity).

sort_method.description: How to sort results. If sort, the values are sorted according to `sort_fields`. If `score`, the value in `sort_fields` is multiplied with the default ElasticSearch score.

sort_reverse.description: Whether or not to reverse the sort applied on `sort_fields`

sort_score_missing.description: The number used in case there is a value missing in your `sort_field`

source_proj.description: By default, the ElasticSearch document is not returned. Specifying `true` will return the entire document. Specifying a `str` or `list(str)` will perform a projection on the document for the specified fields

type.description: The ElasticSearch index you want to query, and the results you expect to get back. `sheet` returns results from Sefaria's user generated source sheets, while `text` returns results from the library.

ShapeJSON.description: The shape API allows one to retrieve information about the shape of an Index on Sefaria. The shape refers some basic statistics about the Index, mostly the number of chapters, and the number of segments per chapter.

Section.description: The parent section for this `Index`. (So for example, the `section` for `Jonah` is `Prophets`. The `section` for `Pesachim` is `Seder Moed`)

isComplex.description: A boolean representing whether or not this is a complex text.

Length.description: The number of chapters or top-level sections in the `Index`.

Chapters.description: For simple texts, this is a list of chapter lengths. For complex texts or categories, the Shape API will return a list of dicts for each text within that category or text.

CatJSON.title: Root Type for CatJSON
CatJSON.description: JSON response for a Category GET request

path.description: The full path of the category

titles.description: The titles for the category, in Hebrew and English.

lastPath.description: The immediate parent of the current category. So for example, with the category `Tanakh/Torah/Genesis`, the `lastPath` is `Torah`.

SheetDetailJSON.title: Root Type for SheetDetailJSON
SheetDetailJSON.description: JSON containing the full content and metadata of a single Sefaria source sheet, including all of its sources.

id.description: Numeric `id` of the sheet — the same value that appears in the sheet's URL on `voices.sefaria.org`.

summary.description: A short, plain-text description of the sheet's contents.

owner.description: Numeric Sefaria user ID of the sheet's owner.

ownerName.description: Display name of the sheet's owner.

ownerProfileUrl.description: Path to the owner's public profile on Sefaria.

ownerImageUrl.description: URL of the owner's profile image.

status.description: Visibility of the sheet — `public` for sheets that are publicly listed, `unlisted` for sheets accessible by direct link only.

sheetLanguage.description: Default display language for the sheet.

dateCreated.description: Timestamp the sheet was first created. May be ISO-8601 or a Unix timestamp string depending on the sheet's age.

dateModified.description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to the sheet.

datePublished.description: Timestamp the sheet was first made public. Empty if the sheet has never been published.

views.description: Number of times the sheet has been viewed.

likes.description: Array of user IDs who have liked the sheet.

options.description: Sheet-level display options used by the Sefaria source sheet editor.

tags.description: Free-text tags applied to the sheet. Often empty in favor of `topics`.

topics.description: Sefaria topics associated with the sheet. Each entry contains the topic `slug`, the typed display value (`asTyped`), and bilingual labels.

displayedCollection.description: Slug of the collection currently displayed alongside this sheet, if any.

collectionName.description: Display name of the collection in `displayedCollection`, if set.

collectionImage.description: URL of the collection's image, if set.

includedRefs.description: All Sefaria refs cited anywhere on the sheet.

expandedRefs.description: Each entry in `includedRefs` expanded out to its constituent segment refs.

nextNode.description: The next available `node` ID — used by the editor when appending a new source.

sources.description: Ordered list of sources that make up the sheet body. Each source has a unique `node` ID and one of several shapes — a Sefaria reference (`ref` + `text`), a free-text comment (`comment`), an arbitrary HTML block (`outsideText`), or a media item (`media`).

node.description: Unique numeric ID for the source within the sheet.

ref.description: Sefaria ref, present on text-source nodes.

outsideText.description: HTML block authored directly on the sheet (not tied to a Sefaria text).

comment.description: Free-text comment authored on the sheet.

media.description: URL of an embedded image or media item.

sheetNotice.description: Optional moderator notice attached to the sheet. `null` when no notice is set.

AuthorIndexesJSON.title: Root Type for AuthorIndexesJSON
AuthorIndexesJSON.description: List of indexes (works) attributed to a given Sefaria author.

title.description: Bilingual display title for the author.

total.description: Total number of indexes attributed to this author.

indexes.description: List of indexes (works) attributed to the author.

categories.description: Sefaria category path for the index.

url.description: Path to the index page on Sefaria.

dependence.description: Whether this index is a base text or a commentary on another text. Empty for base texts.

TrendingSheetTagJSON.title: Root Type for TrendingSheetTagJSON
TrendingSheetTagJSON.description: A single trending sheet tag, including bilingual labels and recent usage counts.

slug.description: URL-safe slug for the tag's underlying topic.

tag.description: English display label for the tag.

he_tag.description: Hebrew display label for the tag.

count.description: Number of recent sheets using this tag.

author_count.description: Number of distinct authors who have used this tag recently.

primaryTitle.description: Bilingual primary title for the tag.

CollectionSummaryJSON.title: Root Type for CollectionSummaryJSON
CollectionSummaryJSON.description: Summary of a Sefaria collection as it appears in collection lists.

headerUrl.description: URL of the collection's header image, if set.

memberCount.description: Number of members in the collection.

sheetCount.description: Number of sheets in the collection.

lastModified.description: Timestamp of the most recent change.

CollectionsListJSON.title: Root Type for CollectionsListJSON
CollectionsListJSON.description: Lists of Sefaria collections separated by visibility. For unauthenticated callers, `private` is empty and `public` contains all publicly-listed collections.

private.description: Private collections visible to the caller. Empty for unauthenticated requests.

CollectionDetailJSON.title: Root Type for CollectionDetailJSON
CollectionDetailJSON.description: Full content of a single Sefaria collection, including its member sheets.

privateSlug.description: Private slug used for collaborator-only access.

lastModified.description: Timestamp of the most recent change.

UserSheetsJSON.title: Root Type for UserSheetsJSON
UserSheetsJSON.description: List of public sheets owned by a user.

sheets.description: Sheets owned by the user. For anonymous requests this contains only public sheets; an authenticated user requesting their own user_id additionally sees their private sheets.
Page of public sheets, each as a summary object.

author.description: Numeric user ID of the sheet owner.

ownerName.description: Display name of the sheet owner.

ownerImageUrl.description: Profile picture URL for the sheet owner. May be an empty string.

ownerProfileUrl.description: Relative URL to the owner's profile page (e.g. `/profile/username`).

ownerOrganization.description: Organization the owner is affiliated with. May be an empty string.

views.description: Number of times the sheet has been viewed.

displayedCollection.description: Slug of the collection this sheet is displayed under, if any. Empty string when not in a collection.

modified.description: Date the sheet was last modified, formatted as `MM/DD/YYYY`.

created.description: ISO 8601 datetime when the sheet was created.

published.description: ISO 8601 datetime when the sheet was made public.

topics.description: Topic tags attached to the sheet.

asTyped.description: The tag label as the author typed it.

en.description: English display name for the topic.

he.description: Hebrew display name for the topic. May be an empty string.

tags.description: Flat list of tag labels (the `asTyped` value for each topic).

options.description: Sheet display options. Empty array when none are set.

BulkSheetEntryJSON.title: Root Type for BulkSheetEntryJSON
BulkSheetEntryJSON.description: Lightweight summary of a single sheet returned by the bulk endpoint.

ref.description: Ordered list of refs, one per aliyah.

SheetTagCountJSON.title: Root Type for SheetTagCountJSON
SheetTagCountJSON.description: A sheet tag and its usage count.

count.description: Number of sheets using this tag.

UserSheetTagJSON.title: Root Type for UserSheetTagJSON
UserSheetTagJSON.description: A topic tag used by a specific user, with bilingual labels.

BulktextJSON.title: Root Type for BulktextJSON
BulktextJSON.description: A map keyed by Sefaria ref. Each entry contains the ref's English and Hebrew text plus metadata such as the language, ref for the url, etc. The exact key set matches the requested refs.

he.description: Hebrew text for the ref. May be an empty string if no Hebrew text is available.

en.description: English text for the ref. May be an empty string if no English text is available.

lang.description: Language of the ref string itself — `he` if the input ref was Hebrew, `en` otherwise.

url.description: URL-safe form of the ref, suitable for constructing a Sefaria link.

primary_category.description: Top-level category of the text. Only present when `useTextFamily=1` is passed.

PassagesJSON.title: Root Type for PassagesJSON
PassagesJSON.description: A map keyed by the requested Sefaria ref. Each value is the canonical passage ref that contains it — for Tanakh a parasha-style passage, for Mishnah a chapter passage, for Talmud a full sugya boundary.

additionalProperties.description: The canonical passage ref that contains the input ref.

IndexTitlesJSON.title: Root Type for IndexTitlesJSON
IndexTitlesJSON.description: The complete list of recognized title strings on Sefaria, including alternate titles and abbreviations across languages. Useful for client-side autocomplete or ref recognition.

IndexJSON.title: Root Type for IndexJSON
IndexJSON.description: Index (work) metadata: title, categories, schema (text structure), order, authors, and descriptive copy.

schema.description: Structural schema describing how the text is divided.

CountsJSON.title: Root Type for CountsJSON
CountsJSON.description: Availability and coverage data for a text, broken down by language. `_all` covers all languages combined; `_he` and `_en` are language-specific. Each language bucket contains availability bitmaps (`availableTexts`), word/segment counts (`availableCounts`), and coverage statistics (`percentAvailable`, `completenessPercent`, `textComplete`, `sparseness`).

_all.description: Coverage across all languages: `availableTexts` (per-chapter availability arrays) and `shape` (structural shape of the text).

availableTexts.description: Nested arrays indicating which segments have text, one array per chapter.

shape.description: Structural shape of the text (chapter/verse counts). Each position represents a chapter (i.e. the first value in the array is the number of verses in chapter 1, the following value in the array is the number of verses in chapter 2)

CountsLinksItemJSON.title: Root Type for CountsLinksItemJSON
CountsLinksItemJSON.description: Link count between two specific books.

CountsWordsJSON.title: Root Type for CountsWordsJSON
CountsWordsJSON.description: Word count for a specific text version.

LinkSummaryJSON.title: Root Type for LinkSummaryJSON
LinkSummaryJSON.description: Single category bucket from a link summary.

books.description: Per-book breakdown within the category, keyed by book title. Each value is the number of links from that book.

CalendarParashaTopicJSON.title: Root Type for CalendarParashaTopicJSON
CalendarParashaTopicJSON.description: Topic metadata for the current week's parasha.

title.description: Bilingual title of the parasha topic.

CalendarHolidayTopicJSON.title: Root Type for CalendarHolidayTopicJSON
CalendarHolidayTopicJSON.description: Topic metadata for an active holiday. Returns 404 when no holiday is in season for the requested date.

ProfileJSON.title: Root Type for ProfileJSON
ProfileJSON.description: Public profile data for a Sefaria user.

slug.description: URL slug used in the profile path.

followers.description: Counts of followers/follow ids; the API returns an empty array on the public endpoint.

followees.description: Counts of followees; empty array on the public endpoint.

CountsLangJSON.title: CountsLangJSON
CountsLangJSON.description: Per-language coverage statistics for a text.

availableTexts.description: Nested arrays indicating which segments have text in this language.

availableCounts.description: Word and segment counts by unit (e.g. chapters, verses).

percentAvailable.description: Percentage of segments that have text in this language.

textComplete.description: Whether the text is considered complete in this language.

sparseness.description: Sparseness level (0 = dense, higher = more gaps).

percentAvailableInvalid.description: True when the percentAvailable figure could not be calculated.

AsyncTaskEnqueued.title: Async Task Enqueued
AsyncTaskEnqueued.description: Response returned when an asynchronous task has been successfully enqueued.

task_id.description: Unique identifier for the enqueued task. Pass this to `GET /api/async/{task_id}` to poll for results.

AsyncTaskPending.title: Async Task Pending
AsyncTaskPending.description: Response returned while a task is still in progress.

state.description: Current state of the task. `PENDING` means not yet started; `STARTED` means actively running; `RETRY` means the task failed and is being retried.

ready.description: Always `false` while the task is in progress.

meta.description: Optional progress or metadata reported by the task while it is running.

AsyncTaskSuccess.title: Async Task Success
AsyncTaskSuccess.description: Response returned when a task has completed successfully. The shape of `result` depends on which API enqueued the task.

ready.description: Always `true` when the task has completed.

result.description: The task output. Its shape depends on which API enqueued the task. For `POST /api/find-refs` tasks, this is a `FindRefsAPIResponse` object.

AsyncTaskFailure.title: Async Task Failure
AsyncTaskFailure.description: Response returned when a task has failed permanently.

ready.description: Always `true` when the task has failed permanently.

error.description: Human-readable description of the error that caused the task to fail.
